data.title: Axogen falls 16% after FDA delays Avance Nerve Graft decision to December
data.description:
Axogen (NASDAQ:AXGN) shares fell 16% in premarket trading after the company announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration had extended the Prescription Drug User Fee Act goal date for its biologics license application for Avance Nerve Graft by three months.

On August 22, the company received a communication from the FDA indicating that the information submitted by the company in response to an FDA information request, which included substantial new manufacturing and facility data not previously reviewed by the agency, was deemed a major amendment to its application. 

Under FDA guidelines, this designation allows for additional time to review the submission. As a result, the FDA has extended the PDUFA goal date to December 5, 2025.

The FDA also informed Axogen (NASDAQ:AXGN) that it now anticipates providing feedback on product labeling in November 2025, consistent with PDUFA review procedures.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: PDD Holdings beats Q2 earnings and revenue estimates
NAPCO Security GAAP EPS of $0.33 beats by $0.06, revenue of $50.7M beats by $6.25M
data.description:
• NAPCO Security press release (NASDAQ:NSSC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.33 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $50.7M (+0.7% Y/Y) beats by $6.25M.
  • Recurring service revenue ("RSR") increased 10% and 14% to $22.4 million and $86.3 million for Q4 and the full year respectively, with a 91% gross margin for both periods

data.title: Timken names Honeywell exec Lucian Boldea as CEO
data.description:
The Timken Company (NYSE:TKR) on Monday said that its Board of Directors has appointed Lucian Boldea the company's president and CEO and member of the Board, effective Sept. 1.

Boldea will succeed Richard G. Kyle, who has been in an interim role since March after having previously served as president and CEO from 2014 to 2024.

Boldea joins Timken (NYSE:TKR) from Honeywell (HON), where he served as President and CEO of Industrial Automation.

data.title: Abercrombie & Fitch announces a fashion partnership with the NFL
data.description:
Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF) announced on Monday a multi-year partnership with The National Football League that makes the retailer the league's first Official NFL Fashion Partner. Men’s and women’s apparel, including hoodies, sweatshirts, t-shirts, outerwear, and accessories representing all 32 NFL teams, is now available in select Abercrombie stores and online.

Building on the success since launching licensed NFL products in 2022, Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF) said its aim is to redefine fan style through a fashion-first lens in partnership with the NFL. To celebrate the partnership, Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) debuted a national seasonal campaign "Style Concierge" featuring players known for their personal style off the field, including Christian McCaffrey (San Francisco 49ers), Amon-Ra St. Brown (Detroit Lions), CeeDee Lamb (Dallas Cowboys), and Tee Higgins (Cincinnati Bengals).

Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) CEO Fran Horowitz said the company is uniquely positioned for this moment where sports, fashion, and culture are converging like never before.

"By pairing athlete-led campaigns and player-designed apparel with a lifestyle assortment, Abercrombie will help blur the line between outfits worn during game day arrivals and everyday wear for both female and male fans as well as for players. In addition, Abercrombie will activate across NFL tentpole events, including select NFL international games and within its retail locations worldwide," highlighted the company.

Shares of Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) were up 0.6% in premarket trading. The mall stock is down 34.1% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Faraday Future highlights the benefits of its crypto portfolio
data.description:
Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (NASDAQ:FFAI) issued another update to shareholders in the form of a letter from Founder and Co-CEO YT Jia.

The electric vehicle maker outlined strategic progress anchored in its "Dual Flywheel & Dual Bridge" ecosystem, which is designed to strengthen the company's core rather than divert its focus. Recognizing the long-cycle and capital-intensive nature of automotive manufacturing, the company introduced the C10 Treasury, a portfolio of leading cryptocurrencies positioned to provide ongoing financial support for Faraday's (NASDAQ:FFAI) main electric vehicle business through capital growth and liquidity. "By accumulating and compounding the world’s Top 10 crypto coin portfolio, it has the potential to provide continuous and sustained capital support for our main business," highlighted Jia.

On the operations side, Faraday (FFAI) highlighted multi-sector engagement after the "Dual Flywheel + Dual Bridge" event, which significantly expanded its industry profile and influence, prompting intensified marketing efforts moving forward. On the product front, the FX Super One was noted to have recently passed an initial safety development milestone, meeting U.S. Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards, a key requirement for access to prominent global markets. At the Hanford manufacturing facility, 60% of the production process planning for the FX Super One is complete, with ongoing process validation and workforce training underway.

Financially, the first $30 million commitment to the C10 Treasury closed, with allocations set to begin next week. The investments include 80% deployed passively via the C10 Index and  20% actively managed.

 Looking ahead, Faraday (FFAI) pointed to its regional Super One launch campaigns, a notable showcase at Burning Man, upcoming sales events in Massachusetts and New York, and the first Go-Global Stand-Up Comedy Festival at FF headquarters.

Shares of Faraday Future (FFAI) are up 12.3% over the last six weeks.

data.title: PDD Holdings Non-GAAP EPADS of $3.08 beats by $1.02, revenue of $14.52B beats by $160M
data.description:
• PDD Holdings press release (NASDAQ:PDD): Q2 Non-GAAP EPADS of $3.08 beats by $1.02.

data.title: Sasol reports FY results
data.description:
• Sasol press release (NYSE:SSL): FY turnover of R249B, down 9% Y/Y

  • Basic earnings per share increased by more than 100% to R10,60 per share compared to a loss per share of R69,94 in the prior year.
  • Shares +7% PM.

data.title: Scienture Holdings files to sell 2.1M shares for holders
data.description:
• Scienture Holdings (NASDAQ:SCNX) on Friday filed a registration statement related to the resale of up to 2,097,317 company shares by certain selling stockholders.
  • The company will not receive any proceeds from the resale of the shares in this offering.

data.title: Royalty Pharma to buy royalty interest in Amgen’s Imdelltra for up to $950M
data.description:
BeOne Medicines (NASDAQ:ONC) is selling its royalty rights on the worldwide sales, excluding China, of Amgen’s (NASDAQ:AMGN) cancer drug Imdelltra (tarlatamab-dlle) for up to $950M to Royalty Pharma (NASDAQ:RPRX).

Imdelltra is an immunotherapy drug approved in the U.S. for patients with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) who have progressed on or after receiving platinum-based chemotherapy.

Under the terms of the agreement, BeOne will receive an upfront payment of $885M, with the option to sell remaining royalties within 12 months for up to $65M.

The company will share in a portion of the royalty on annual sales above $1.5B, and will maintain royalty and all other rights to other assets under the terms of the existing collaboration with Amgen, including xaluritamig.

Source: Press Release
